{{Quote|That will be my ship.|Natasi Daala, seeing ''Night Hammer'' for the first time|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
The '''''Knight Hammer''''', originally named the '''''Night Hammer''''', was a black [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] constructed in secret by [[Superior General]] [[Sander Delvardus]] and appropriated by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] after Delvardus's [[Death/Legends|death]] [[Imperial Reunification|at her hands]]. Covered in stealth [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and hosting automated systems that allowed it to run with a fraction of the standard crew size, the ''Knight Hammer'' was particularly advanced for [[Starship/Legends|ships]] of its class. Daala used the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] as a symbol of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] power, reuniting the [[Imperial Remnant|remnants]] of the Empire under its banner as she prepared for an assault against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. When she decided to use the vessel against the [[New Jedi Order]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], she rechristened the ship from ''Night Hammer'' to ''Knight Hammer'' as a private joke and symbol of its intended role in eliminating the New Republic and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].

Daala's [[Assault on Yavin 4|attack on Yavin 4]] was thrown awry when the vanguard [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] under the command of [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] was [[Telekinesis/Legends|pushed]] out of the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] by the Jedi amassed on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] below. Arriving shortly after, the ''Knight Hammer'' [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] the moon and the Jedi facilities. The arrival of the New Republic [[MC90 Star Cruiser|MC90 cruiser]] ''[[Galactic Voyager]]'' under the command of Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] made the ''Knight Hammer'' divert its fire from the Jedi, but the Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] firepower and Daala's reinforcements were able to hold the Republic forces at bay. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Callista Ming]], however, infiltrated her way aboard the ship and rigged its complement of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] to fire their [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] into the ''Knight Hammer''{{'s}} [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. With its engines crippled, the ''Knight Hammer'' fell into the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]] and was destroyed.

==Description==
{{Quote|Only the ''Executor'' was this big—and that one ship practically bankrupted the Empire.|Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
An [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]],<ref name="Darksaber" /> the ''Knight Hammer'' was advanced for [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] of its class.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Plated with heavy black stealth [[Armor/Legends|armor]], the vessel was invisible to enemy forces both sensor wise as well as visually at all but the closest ranges.<ref name="Darksaber" /> Measuring 19 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]],<ref name="Length" /> it was the equal of twenty [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], wielding incredible firepower and having few vulnerabilities. The combination of its size and color gave the ''Knight Hammer'' potent psychological value, with its [[Commander/Legends|commander]] able to use it to demoralize enemies. It was a symbol of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] might, the most powerful [[Starship/Legends|ship]] in its [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. The scale of the ship had drawbacks as well, though; the ''Knight Hammer'' was both very expensive and cumbersome. It had at least 293 decks, and [[Turbolift/Legends|transport tubes]] ran through the ship to aid in moving around. Simple schematics of the ''Knight Hammer'' were provided throughout the vessel to help new recruits orient themselves in the massive ship.<ref name="Darksaber" />

The ''Knight Hammer'' could carry a crew of up to one hundred thousand, a relatively small crew for its size. This was made possible by a network of highly redundant automated command systems. It could hold enough forces to serve as an occupation force on a conquered [[Planet/Legends|world]]. Thousands of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] filled its [[Hangar/Legends|launching bays]], with certain bays in the aft of the ship, directly beside the massive [[Engine/Legends|engine]] systems, dedicated to [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]. Hundreds of [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] were available in case an evacuation was required.<ref name="Darksaber" />

The ''Knight Hammer'' featured an extravagant [[Ready room/Legends|ready room]], approximately the size of the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|command deck]] of a [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]], in which the commander of the vessel could privately strategize. It came with its own sleeping quarters, was equipped with food processing stations, and had access to escape pods for the ship's command-level staff. A viewing window looked out onto the [[Star/Legends|stars]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

==History==
===Commissioning===
Following its defeat in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Galactic Empire fragmented as a number of [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] broke away and founded independent kingdoms. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], one of them, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sander Delvardus]], was driven from [[Eriadu Authority|his holdings]] around [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] into the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] by [[Sien Sovv]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="TEA" /> There he claimed the title "[[Superior General]]," established his own personal satrapy, and began construction on a Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the ''Night Hammer''.<ref name="Darksaber" /> It was rumored that Delvardus called the ship ''Night Hammer'' in honor of his fallen love, [[Seledra-Zin]], who Delvardus had put in a [[Coma/Legends|coma]] after striking her with a [[Dynamic hammer/Legends|dynamic hammer]] in a fit of rage.<ref name="Star Wars Blog">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> Delvardus funneled away [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from the rest of what remained of the Empire, placing almost all of them into the massive ship's development. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Admiral [[Natasi Daala]] had Delvardus and twelve more of the most powerful Deep Core warlords [[Death/Legends|killed]] to prevent them from tearing apart Imperial unity. Upon investigating Delvardus's assets and finding that the warlord had siphoned away a large number of Imperial credits, Daala went to [[Kampe|his fortress world]] to speak with his [[Executive officer/Legends|second in command]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Ivan Cronus]]. Cronus took her to the edge of the planet's [[Star system/Legends|system]] where ''Night Hammer'' waited, and upon first seeing the vessel, Daala claimed it as her [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

Daala at first used the ''Night Hammer'' in her effort to reunite the fragments of the Empire, taking the ship from system to system in order to secure loyalties, forge alliances, and motivate the subjects of the Empire to work harder. Around her new ship, Daala gathered 112 ''Victory''-class Star Destroyers and another 45 ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers; the ''Night Hammer'' and its accompanying fleet remained docked at a nexus [[Space station/Legends|station]] in the Deep Core as Daala prepared to take action with her new forces. She met with [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] in the ''Night Hammer''{{'s}} ready room to select their first target; after concluding that [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was too difficult a fight and putting it off as a later potential target, Daala decided to eliminate the [[New Jedi Order]] at their [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. With the ''Victories'', Cronus would occupy the New Republic's attention elsewhere, while Pellaeon would lead the first wave of the [[Assault on Yavin 4|attack on Yavin]] with the ''Imperials'' and Daala would follow in the ''Night Hammer'' to secure the system.<ref name="Darksaber" />

When two [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], [[Kyp Durron]] and [[Dorsk 81]], infiltrated Daala's muster of the fleet and overheard her plans, the Admiral was forced to move up her schedule. Cronus was dispatched to [[Attack on Khomm|wreak havoc]] on [[Khomm/Legends|Khomm]] and elsewhere, while Pellaeon was sent directly to Yavin 4 with his fleet of seventeen ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers. Aboard ''Night Hammer'', Daala gathered enough of her forces to permanently occupy the praxeum. Daala's final order before launching was to rechristen the ship ''Knight Hammer'', as a symbol of her planned destruction of the New Republic through the annihilation of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

===The battle of Yavin 4===
{{Quote|That's Daala. She doesn't even know what she's firing at.|Han Solo|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
[[File:Jediacademi_01_20.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Pellaeon's initial attack is driven back.]]
Having been warned by the Jedi about the impending attack, Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] of the New Republic took his portion of the fleet, including his flagship, the [[MC90 Star Cruiser|MC90 cruiser]] ''[[Galactic Voyager]]'', and headed for Yavin 4. Meanwhile, Pellaeon had encountered a setback; the Jedi had combined forces to [[Telekinesis/Legends|push]] his entire fleet out of the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. When the ''Knight Hammer'' arrived in-system, it was to find no forces around Yavin 4. Daala initiated a scan of the system for Pellaeon's fleet, but to no avail. Unwilling to commit her [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighters]] to a battle where the enemy had resources she had not anticipated, Daala ordered the ''Knight Hammer''{{'s}} flights of TIE [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] and bombers to stand down. Instead, the ''Knight Hammer'' jammed communications, dropped into orbit around Yavin 4, and [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|opened fire]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] with all its [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, targeting Jedi structures and setting the surrounding forests aflame.<ref name="Darksaber" />

The [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], carrying [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Jedi Knight [[Callista Ming]], among others, arrived in the Yavin system mid-bombardment, and the ''Knight Hammer'' turned some of its guns on the other ship. As the ''Falcon'' avoided its fire, the ''Knight Hammer'' dispatched its TIE fighters to bring down the smaller vessel. However, the ''Falcon'' was able to evade them, too, and got to ground on the moon below, where Skywalker aided in fighting off the Imperial ground forces. Ming, meanwhile, hijacked a TIE bomber and flew it back up to the ''Knight Hammer'', where she faked damage and was granted entry into the Super Star Destroyer's rear bomber bay, still holding its full complement of bombers.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Meanwhile, the ''Falcon'', under the command of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], headed back into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] to make a run at the ''Knight Hammer''. The Super Star Destroyer ceased firing on the moon below and turned all guns on the ''Falcon'', then launched four squadrons from its forward hangar against the ship. It was at this point that Ackbar's fleet arrived, consisting of the ''Galactic Voyager'' and four [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]]. As the New Republic forces sent the TIE fighters into retreat back to the Super Star Destroyer and provided sanctuary for the ''Falcon'', the ''Knight Hammer'' opened fire on the ''Voyager''. Ackbar intended to hold off Daala until New Republic reinforcements reached Yavin, but before he had a chance, Cronus and his fleet of ''Victory''-class Star Destroyers arrived in system to join forces with Daala. Catching the New Republic ships off guard, Cronus's fleet took out one of Ackbar's [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]]. The ''Knight Hammer'' began indiscriminately firing into the battle, catching one of Cronus's ships in the crossfire. One more corvette was taken out, and Cronus himself was killed when a damaged corvette [[Ramming/Legends|piloted itself]] into his [[13X|flagship]]. The ''Galactic Voyager'' broke away from the moon and headed for the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]], and the ''Knight Hammer'' pursued. As they closed on Ackbar's vessel, Daala got word from Pellaeon outside the system, where his ships had at last settled.<ref name="Darksaber" />

As the battle outside was taking place, Ming locked out the ''Knight Hammer''{{'s}} personnel from the bay she was docked in and began rigging each bomber to automatically fire its [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] into the bulkhead separating the bay from the engines. She then fled the bay via transport tube, heading for the ship's bridge. As she neared it, the timer ran out and the bombers opened fire. A volley of [[Missile/Legends|missile]] explosions ripped open the aft end of the ''Knight Hammer'', and the Super Star Destroyer's engines went dead. The power throughout the vessel dropped, a shockwave rippled its way toward the bow, and the ship went dead in space and began to drift directly into the gas giant. The entire rear third of the ship sealed itself off as [[Life support/Legends|life support]] failed throughout the vessel and [[Poison/Legends|toxins]] began leaking into the habitable areas.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Out among Daala's fleet, panic broke out as the Imperials witnessed the extensive damage done to the ''Knight Hammer''. Daala called for assistance from Cronus, but upon learning that he was dead, she was forced to reassess the situation. She realized the ''Knight Hammer'' was irreparable in the time she had left, and was doomed to fall into Yavin; what was more, the rest of the New Republic fleet arrived in that moment, vastly outnumbering her remaining forces. At that, Daala ordered an evacuation of the ''Knight Hammer''. She remained behind as her bridge crew left, letting them believe she was going down with the ship, then headed for the command escape pods in her ready room. However, she was stopped by Callista Ming, who intended to delay Daala long enough to doom her in the ship's death. After fruitlessly shooting at her with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and having her shots deflected by Ming's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], Daala hit Ming with a [[Stun/Legends|stun]] blast, knocking the Jedi unconscious. As the ''Knight Hammer'' fell into the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of Yavin, Daala evacuated the doomed vessel. Ming recovered and followed shortly after. The ''Knight Hammer'', heated by atmospheric friction and crushed by pressure, exploded as it hurtled into the depths of the gas giant.<ref name="Darksaber" />

==Commanders and crew==
Though the ''Night Hammer'' was built for Sander Delvardus, Natasi Daala was its first and only commander. Despite her preference for a smaller command post, Daala believed that an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of her position could have no lesser a ship than the Super Star Destroyer. She used the vessel as a psychological weapon, expecting its arrival in a battle to be a blow to the opposing side's morale. Her renaming of the vessel was symbolic of the destruction she hoped it would inflict on the New Republic and Jedi, and to Daala proved she had a sense of humor. The Admiral placed stock in her image and mystique, letting her crew believe she was going down with her ship when the vessel was doomed.<ref name="Darksaber" />

With a maximum complement of one hundred thousand, the ''Knight Hammer''{{'s}} crew was small for a ship of its size. Despite the usual Imperial policy against [[Gender/Legends|females]] in the military, the vessel under Daala had female [[Officer/Legends|officers]] in its bridge crew. Throughout her travels around the remnants of the Empire in the Super Star Destroyer, Daala was also accompanied by a detachment of [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guards]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Knight Hammer'' was created for [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[1995]] novel [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]. ''Darksaber'' declares the ship to be as large as the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], which as of 1995 was eight kilometers; the novel, in fact, also specifically states the ship is eight kilometers long. Since then, however, the ''Executor'' and all ships of its class have been [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] to nineteen kilometers long.<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20060309jc84|series=Jedi Counseling|text=84}}</ref>
